content.full_text:
March 26, 1992

Scott Hallabrin  
Acting General Counsel  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 "J" Street, Suite 800  
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: Potential Conflict of Interest for City Hall Project

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

This letter confirms a March 20, 1992 teleconference between Margaret Ellison of your office and myself. The Office of the City Attorney seeks an informal advice letter relating to a potential conflict of interest concerning the procurement of a base isolation system for the City Hall Earthquake Repair Project.

As the attached opinion from our office indicates, there does not appear to be a conflict of interest pursuant to federal or state law. However, we seek confirmation of this by your office.

In the teleconference with your Ms. Ellison, she indicated that, in all likelihood, your office would not render an informal opinion because the conflict, if any, has already occurred. We disagree with this position because the award of the contract has not yet occurred. Hence, we seek prospective advice. We'd appreciate an opportunity to discuss this at your earliest convenience.

The City Hall Project is on a fast-track and the selection of a base isolation system will become a critical path item within four (4) weeks. Accordingly, the City requests that an informal advice letter be provided within that time period.

Scott Hallabrin  
March 26, 1992  
Page 2

Thank you in advance for your prompt attention in this matter.

Very truly yours,

JAYNE W. WILLIAMS  
City Attorney

By: [signature]  
MARK P. WALD  
Assistant to the City Attorney

MPW:jrs  
Enclosure

cc: John Soderling, Office of Public Works  
     Ezra Rapport, Office of the City Manager
